name: Focaccia (Italian Flatbread)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, mix together 1 1/2 cups of flour, rosemary, salt, and yeast.
2. Add the 3 tablespoons of olive oil and hot water to the dry ingredients. Mix on low speed for 30 seconds, scraping the sides of the bowl frequently.
3. Continue mixing on medium speed for 1 minute. Stir in enough remaining flour to make a dough that is easy to handle.
4. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface. Knead for 5-8 minutes until smooth and elastic.
5. Place the dough in a greased bowl, turning the greased side up. Cover and let rise in a warm place for about 1 hour, or until doubled in size (the dough is ready if the indentation remains when touched).
6. Punch down the dough. Brush a 12-inch pizza pan or large cookie sheet with olive oil.
7. Press the dough into the pan or flatten it into a 12-inch circle on the cookie sheet. Make indentations about 2 inches apart using your fingers.
8. Brush the dough with olive oil and sprinkle with pepper. Let rise uncovered in a warm place for an additional 30 minutes.
9. Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
10. Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown. Brush with additional oil before serving.
If using quick-acting yeast, omit the first rising period. After kneading, cover the dough and let rest for 10 minutes. Then proceed with pressing the dough into the pan.
High altitude directions (3500 to 6500 feet): Rising times may be slightly shorter.
